searches_performed.query: dong quai warfarin INR bleeding case report coumarin Angelica sinensis interaction
searches_performed.notes: Primary evidence: Page 1999 case report. Additional: Chinese-herbal-warfarin reviews + warfarin-herb interaction syntheses.

searches_with_no_results.query: dong quai warfarin randomized controlled trial
searches_with_no_results.notes: No RCT exists. Evidence is a single case report + animal PK/PD data + mechanism (coumarin content).

sources.title: Potentiation of warfarin by dong quai
sources.population: 46-year-old African-American woman with atrial fibrillation, stable on warfarin
sources.design_notes: Single case report. Dong quai 565 mg 1-2 times daily for 4 weeks alongside warfarin.
sources.key_findings: INR rose from therapeutic range to 4.05, then 4.90 (both greater than 2x baseline). No bleeding events. INR returned to acceptable levels 1 month after discontinuing dong quai. No other changes in diet, medication, or health status were identified.
sources.limitations: Single case. Dose not fully standardized (OTC product). Temporal association does not prove causation, though the washout-rechallenge pattern strengthens attribution.

sources.title: Warfarin Interactions with Medicinal Herbs
sources.population: Review of herb-warfarin case reports and PK studies
sources.key_findings: Dong quai contains natural coumarin derivatives (ferulic acid, osthole) with documented antithrombotic and platelet-inhibitory effects. Animal steady-state studies show dong quai significantly increased prothrombin time WITHOUT affecting warfarin concentration - suggesting pharmacodynamic (not pharmacokinetic) interaction. Recommends informing patients of the potential hazard.

sources.title: Interaction between warfarin and Chinese herbal medicines
sources.design_notes: Reviews pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic interactions of Chinese herbal medicines with warfarin.
sources.key_findings: Dong quai listed among Chinese herbal medicines with documented warfarin potentiation via PD mechanism. Recommends caution when combining.

claims.statement: Dong quai can potentiate warfarin, raising INR and increasing bleeding risk.

dose_threshold.a: Case report used 565 mg 1-2x/day. Lower doses not studied.
dose_threshold.notes: Single case report + animal data only. No RCT.

claims.statement: The proposed mechanism is pharmacodynamic (dong quai coumarin derivatives add to warfarin's anticoagulant effect) rather than pharmacokinetic (warfarin concentration unchanged in animal studies).

dose_threshold.a: Mechanism operates at any dose that provides measurable coumarin content.
dose_threshold.notes: Animal PK studies show warfarin concentration unchanged, so PK-based workarounds do not apply.

claims.statement: Patients on warfarin should not start dong quai without prescriber approval and INR monitoring.

dose_threshold.notes: INR check within 1-2 weeks of starting.

reviewer_notes: Original entry was DANGER, 24 words, 1/2 specific. Evidence base is thin (1 case report + animal data + mechanism), so WARNING more honestly reflects certainty level. The interaction is plausible and worth avoiding; not DANGER-tier because only a single reversible case is documented.
